headline: Political appointees to GLIC boards tripled under unity government
context: Government-linked investment companies saw political appointee board members triple from 18 to 54 between 2022 and 2025 under the unity government's coalition arrangement.

cards.big: Political appointees on GLIC boards tripled to 54 in three years
cards.sub: Unity government coalition partners each demanded board representation as political reward

cards.big: GLC Transformation Programme 2005 explicitly called for professional boards
cards.sub: Current appointment pattern directly contradicts 20-year-old reform commitments

cards.big: GLICs manage RM1.9 trillion in combined assets — pensions, savings, investments
cards.sub: Political interference in investment decisions risks retirement savings of millions

cards.big: EPF, KWAP, and PNB all received new politically-connected directors in 2025
cards.sub: Institutional investors' independence compromised by coalition horse-trading

cards.big: When board seats are political currency, public money becomes coalition glue
Should GLIC board appointments require approval from an independent nominations committee?
